pullRequest.title: Add support for using cert manager to generate webhook certificates
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:

4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes. Explain the motivation behind these changes and link to relevant issues or discussions. -->

Close #1178 

**Proposed changes:**

- Will use cert manager to generate TLS certificates if `certManager.enable` is `true`
- webhook should sync the certs into local file once the webhook TLS secret has been renewed

pullRequest.title: Make logging encoder configurable
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, check our contributor guidelines: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/about/contributing
2. To know more about how to develop with the Spark operator, check the developer guide: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/spark-operator/developer-guide/
3. If you want *faster* PR reviews, check how: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md#best-practices-for-faster-reviews
4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes. Explain the motivation behind these changes and link to relevant issues or discussions. -->

Close #2254.

**Proposed changes:**

1. Make logging encoder configurable by adding new values `controller.logEncoder` and `webhook.logEncoder` to Helm chart (defaults to `console`).

2. When `{controller,webhook}.logEncoder` is set to `console`, the logs will be like:

```txt
2025-07-04T14:08:33.090Z	INFO	sparkapplication/controller.go:180	Reconciling SparkApplication	{"controller": "spark-application-controller", "namespace": "spark", "name": "spark-pi", "reconcileID": "4552e91c-7ab3-4983-9ea6-27e30761339f", "state": "COMPLETED"}
2025-07-04T14:08:33.126Z	INFO	sparkapplication/controller.go:205	Finished reconciling SparkApplication	{"controller": "spark-application-controller", "namespace": "spark", "name": "spark-pi", "reconcileID": "4552e91c-7ab3-4983-9ea6-27e30761339f"}
```

And when `{controller,webhook}.logEncoder` is  set to`json`, the logs will be like:

```json
{"level":"INFO","ts":"2025-07-04T14:09:01.818Z","caller":"sparkapplication/controller.go:180","msg":"Reconciling SparkApplication","controller":"spark-application-controller","namespace":"spark","name":"spark-pi","reconcileID":"8e6a7bb7-8f1a-456e-abf7-f206efae1a34","state":"COMPLETED"}
{"level":"INFO","ts":"2025-07-04T14:09:01.850Z","caller":"sparkapplication/controller.go:205","msg":"Finished reconciling SparkApplication","controller":"spark-application-controller","namespace":"spark","name":"spark-pi","reconcileID":"8e6a7bb7-8f1a-456e-abf7-f206efae1a34"}
```

3. Disable colored logging level, making it easier to collect and parse logs.

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

<!-- Provide reasoning for the changes if not already covered in the description above. -->

pullRequest.title: Suspend/Resume feature on SparkApplication
pullRequest.body:
## Purpose of this PR

This PR implements Suspend/Resume operation on SparkApplication.

**Proposed changes:**

- Add `Suspend: true|false` field in `SparkApplicationSpec`
- State Transition:
  - Suspend: `**` --`Suspend: true`--> `SUSPENDING` --> `SUSPENDED` (`**`=_any non-Terminal Application State_)
  - Resume: `SUSPENDING | SUSPENDED` --`Suspend: false`--> `RESUMING` --> `SUBMITTED`

Fixes #2290 

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

Supporting Suspend/Resume feature could make Kueue integration easier.

## Checklist

<!-- Before submitting your PR, please review the following: -->

- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.

<!-- Include any additional notes or context that could be helpful for the reviewers here. -->

pullRequest.title: Make default ingress tls and annotations congurable in the helm config
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, check our contributor guidelines: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/about/contributing
2. To know more about how to develop with the Spark operator, check the developer guide: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/spark-operator/developer-guide/
3. If you want *faster* PR reviews, check how: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md#best-practices-for-faster-reviews
4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR
Support helm chart level defaults for ingress TLS and annotations, so they don't need to be configured on every SparkApplication. 
Closes: https://github.com/kubeflow/spark-operator/issues/2460

**Proposed changes:**

- Add CLI arguments `--ingress-tls` and `--ingress-annotations` and use them when TLS/annotations are not provided in the SparkApplication's spec. These are provided as JSON strings in the CLI. 
- Expose these via the helm config. The user configures them as normal in the helm yaml and they get serialised to JSON, so they can be read by the controller. 
- Wrote unit tests. 

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

<!-- Provide reasoning for the changes if not already covered in the description above. -->

## Checklist

<!-- Before submitting your PR, please review the following: -->

- [x] I have conducted a self-review of my own code.
- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

### Additional Notes
I considered adding an e2e test to cover the helm and CLI configuration. However, I couldn't see a way to run the e2e tests with multiple helm configs. I could have changed all the e2e tests to use these new configs, but then we loose test coverage for when the new configs are not used. 

I'm a golang noob.

pullRequest.title: feat: watch namespaces based on labels
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, check our contributor guidelines: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/about/contributing
2. To know more about how to develop with the Spark operator, check the developer guide: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/spark-operator/developer-guide/
3. If you want *faster* PR reviews, check how: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md#best-practices-for-faster-reviews
4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR

This PR implements namespace selector functionality, allowing the Spark Operator to watch namespaces based on Kubernetes label selectors in addition to explicit namespace lists.

Closes #2479.

**Proposed changes:**

- **Core Implementation**
   - **New `NamespaceMatcher` utility** (`pkg/util/namespace.go`): Handles matching namespaces against explicit lists and label selectors
   - **CLI flag**: Added `--namespace-selector` flag accepting kubectl-style label selector syntax
   - **Dynamic filtering**: Event filters now check namespace labels at runtime via cache lookups
   - **Cache configuration**: When selector is used, operator caches all namespaces for dynamic filtering
- **Controller Updates**
   - **SparkApplication**: Updated to support namespace selector
   - **ScheduledSparkApplication**: Updated to support namespace selector  
   - **SparkConnect**: Updated to support namespace selector
- **Helm Chart**
   - **New value**: `spark.jobNamespaceSelector` for configuring label selector
   - **Conditional RBAC**: Namespace list/watch permissions added only when selector is configured
   - **Documentation**: Added inline documentation for selector syntax and requirements

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

In multi-tenant Kubernetes environments, managing an explicit list of namespaces can be operationally burdensome. Every time a new namespace needs to be onboarded, the operator deployment must be updated. This PR enables watching namespaces based on labels, eliminating the need for operator restarts when namespaces are added or removed.

## Checklist

<!-- Before submitting your PR, please review the following: -->

- [x] I have conducted a self-review of my own code.
- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

## Updated Implementation (Jan 25, 2026)

Force-pushed updates to clean up the implementation and add test coverage.

### Fixed
- RBAC Helm template logic for conditional cluster-scoped permissions
- Namespace matcher utility error handling and edge cases

### Added
- Integration tests covering namespace selector–based filtering

## Additional Notes

<!-- Include any additional notes or context that could be helpful for the reviewers here. -->

pullRequest.title: upgrade to Spark 4.0.0
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, check our contributor guidelines: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/about/contributing
2. To know more about how to develop with the Spark operator, check the developer guide: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/spark-operator/developer-guide/
3. If you want *faster* PR reviews, check how: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md#best-practices-for-faster-reviews
4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR

This PR upgrades Spark to 4.0.0. 

There are some changes to core from 3.5 to 4.0 but as far as I can tell, they don't affect us: https://spark.apache.org/docs/latest/core-migration-guide.html#upgrading-from-core-35-to-40

There hasn't been many changes to spark-submit: https://github.com/apache/spark/commits/master/docs/submitting-applications.md


I do have some concerns:
- are we going to maintain a separate branch for spark 3 for those who haven't or cannot upgrade to 4 yet?
- if yes, do we need a separate release process / automation?

fixes: #2538 

**Proposed changes:**

- Update Dockerfile and examples to reference 4.0.0.
- Update Docker image references to use FQDN.

pullRequest.title: feat: add configurable timestamp precision for ScheduledSparkApplication names
pullRequest.body:
<!--  Thanks for sending a pull request! Here are some tips for you:
1. If this is your first time, check our contributor guidelines: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/about/contributing
2. To know more about how to develop with the Spark operator, check the developer guide: https://www.kubeflow.org/docs/components/spark-operator/developer-guide/
3. If you want *faster* PR reviews, check how: https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md#best-practices-for-faster-reviews
4. Please open an issue to discuss significant work before you start. We appreciate your contributions and don't want your efforts to go to waste!
-->

## Purpose of this PR
Added a global controller option to configure the timestamp precision used when generating SparkApplication names from ScheduledSparkApplications. This addresses the issue where nanosecond timestamps (19 digits) combined with long application names can exceed Kubernetes' 63-character name limit.
<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes. Explain the motivation behind these changes and link to relevant issues or discussions. -->

Fixes #2602 

**Proposed changes:**


- Add `--scheduled-spark-application-timestamp-precision` flag to controller with valid values: `nanos` (default), `micros`, `millis`, `seconds`, `minutes`
- Add formatTimestamp() helper function and update createSparkApplication() to use configurable precision
- Add `controller.scheduledSparkApplicationTimestampPrecision` Helm chart value and pass it to controller deployment
- Add unit tests for all precision levels

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)

### Rationale

<!-- Provide reasoning for the changes if not already covered in the description above. -->
When using ScheduledSparkApplication with long names, the generated SparkApplication names can exceed Kubernetes' 63-character limit due to the 19-digit nanosecond timestamp suffix. This feature allows operators to configure shorter timestamp precisions (e.g., `seconds` = 10 digits, `minutes` = 8 digits) to stay within the limit while maintaining uniqueness for their scheduling frequency.
The default remains `nanos` for full backward compatibility.

## Checklist

- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

pullRequest.title: fix: avoid duplicate web UI service/ingress creation during reconciliation
pullRequest.body:
## Purpose of this PR

Fixes #2701

While in `Submitted` state, reconciliation is triggered multiple times (driver pod created, pod phase transitions, status updates). Each reconciliation re-attempted `Create()` on the same web UI service and ingress, causing:
- Wasteful API calls (failed Create + Update) on every cycle
- Kyverno policy violations from the failed Create attempts before `AlreadyExists` is returned
- Potential zombie SparkApplications stuck in Submitted state

**Proposed changes:**

- Add a status guard (`WebUIServiceName == ""`) in `reconcileSubmittedSparkApplication` to skip web UI resource creation when resources were already created in a previous reconciliation
- Switch from `Create → catch AlreadyExists → Update` to `Get → if NotFound → Create, else → Update` pattern in `createDriverIngressService`, `createDriverIngressV1`, and `createDriverIngressLegacy` to avoid unnecessary failed API calls that trigger Kyverno policy violations

## Change Category

- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

The root cause is that `reconcileSubmittedSparkApplication` has no guard before creating web UI resources. Every reconciliation while in Submitted state re-runs the creation logic. The old `Create → catch AlreadyExists` pattern generates a failed Create API call before falling through to Update. With Kyverno policies, this failed Create triggers a policy violation before the `AlreadyExists` error is returned — causing the conflict reported in #2701.

The fix addresses both layers:
1. **Status guard**: Once `WebUIServiceName` is set in status, subsequent reconciliations skip the creation block entirely — zero unnecessary API calls
2. **Get-then-Create**: Even for `DriverIngressOptions` (which don't have a status guard), the Get-first pattern avoids the failed Create that triggers Kyverno violations. The Update path now properly merges onto the existing object (preserving `ResourceVersion`)

## Checklist

- [x] I have conducted a self-review of my own code.
- [ ] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

### Additional Notes

- All existing unit tests pass
- All 13 e2e tests pass on Kind cluster (k8s v1.32.0)
- All CI checks pass locally: `go mod tidy`, `go-fmt`, `go-vet`, `build-operator`

## Purpose of this PR

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes. Explain the motivation behind these changes and link to relevant issues or discussions. -->

Close #2724.

**Proposed changes:**

- Remove unnecessary error logging for suspended SparkApplication reconciliation.

## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

### Rationale

<!-- Provide reasoning for the changes if not already covered in the description above. -->

- When the app is in `SUSPENDING` state, the controller sends requests to API server to delete resources like driver pod, server and ingress, then change the state to `SUSPENDED` state.
- But the driver pod can still exists when reconciling a `SUSPENDED` app, thus the erros will show up in controller logs.
- When the driver pod is actually deleted, the `SUSPENDED` app will be reconciled again. So it is fine to remove the error logging statements to suprress the errors.

pullRequest.title: Fix : Propagate batch scheduler initialization errors to trigger retries
pullRequest.body:
__Description:__
Fixes #2782 

Currently, the `SparkApplication` controller has an issue when handling errors that occur during the initialization or retrieval phase of a batch scheduler (e.g., Volcano) via the `shouldDoBatchScheduling` function. If the `r.registry.GetScheduler` call fails due to underlying Kubernetes API Server issues (e.g., transient errors like "CRD PodGroup does not exist" caused by a temporary `etcd` disconnection), the `shouldDoBatchScheduling` function logs the error but does not propagate it back to the `submitSparkApplication` caller.

This means that even if batch scheduler setup fails, the `submitErr` variable within `submitSparkApplication` remains `nil`. As a result, the `SparkApplication`'s status is not set to `ApplicationStateFailedSubmission`, preventing the built-in retry mechanism from being activated. The application might continue attempting submission with an improperly configured batch scheduler, or the error information might not be correctly reflected in the `SparkApplication`'s status.

__Changes Made:__

1. __Modified `Reconciler.shouldDoBatchScheduling` function signature:__

   - The function now returns an additional `error` type, i.e., `(bool, scheduler.Interface, error)`.
   - When `r.registry.GetScheduler` returns an error, `shouldDoBatchScheduling` will no longer just log the error but will also return it as part of its return values.
   - For robustness, if `GetScheduler` succeeds but returns a `nil` scheduler object, a corresponding error is also returned.

2. __Modified `Reconciler.submitSparkApplication` function:__

   - It now captures the error returned by `r.shouldDoBatchScheduling`.
   - If `shouldDoBatchScheduling` returns an error, `submitSparkApplication` will assign this error to `submitErr` and return early.
   - The logic in the `defer` statement will detect the presence of `submitErr`, correctly updating the `SparkApplication`'s status to `ApplicationStateFailedSubmission` and logging the appropriate failure message.

__Problem Solved:__

- __Error Propagation:__ Ensures that errors occurring during batch scheduler initialization or retrieval are correctly propagated upstream.
- __Retry Mechanism Activation:__ Enables submission failures caused by batch scheduler configuration or temporary API issues to trigger the `SparkApplication`'s built-in `FailedSubmission` retry strategy. This enhances resilience to transient errors.
- __Status Accuracy:__ Guarantees that the `SparkApplication`'s status accurately reflects the batch scheduler setup failure, providing clearer feedback to users.

__Testing Strategy:__

- Simulate transient Kubernetes API Server errors when handling CRD requests (e.g., during `GetScheduler` attempting to get the Volcano client, simulate a non-existent `PodGroup` CRD, or temporary `kube-apiserver` unavailability). Observe if the `SparkApplication` correctly enters the `FailedSubmission` state and retries.
- Confirm that the existing submission process remains unaffected when the batch scheduler initializes successfully.

pullRequest.title: feat: add Python API generation for Spark Operator CRDs
pullRequest.body:
## Purpose of this PR

Adds Python API generation for the Spark Operator, enabling users to programmatically create `SparkApplication`, `ScheduledSparkApplication`, and `SparkConnect` resources using typed Python models generated directly from CRD schemas.

This follows the same pattern established in the Kubeflow Trainer repository.

**Proposed changes:**

- Add OpenAPI generation tooling (`hack/openapi/`, `hack/swagger/`)
- Add Python API generation tooling (`hack/python-api/`)
- Generate OpenAPI spec and Python models for v1alpha1 and v1beta2 APIs
- Add `make python-api` target

## Change Category

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

This change is additive and does not affect existing controller behavior or APIs.

### Rationale

Users currently interact with Spark Operator CRDs via YAML manifests or untyped dictionaries. This PR enables a typed Python experience with IDE autocompletion and validation, consistent with the direction established by Kubeflow Trainer.

While users can use `kubernetes-client/python`, this typically requires constructing untyped dictionaries or manually assembling deeply nested objects without Spark-specific typing or IDE support.

## Checklist

- [x] I have conducted a self-review of my own code.
- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [ ] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

### Additional Notes

**New generated and tooling files:**
| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `hack/openapi/gen-openapi.sh` | Generates OpenAPI definitions from Go types |
| `hack/openapi/violation_exceptions.list` | Suppresses known K8s API rule violations |
| `hack/swagger/main.go` | Combines OpenAPI definitions into swagger.json |
| `hack/python-api/gen-api.sh` | Generates Python models from swagger.json |
| `hack/python-api/swagger_config.json` | OpenAPI generator configuration |
| `api/openapi-spec/swagger.json` | Generated OpenAPI specification |
| `api/python_api/` | Generated Python package |

**API changes:**
- Enabled OpenAPI generation for Spark Operator API packages by adding `+k8s:openapi-gen=true` markers

No new runtime logic or API surface changes are introduced; this PR only adds tooling and generated artifacts.

**Related:**
- Closes #2818

pullRequest.title: fix: handle nil Executor.Instances in GetExecutorRequestResource
pullRequest.body:
Fixes #2832


## Purpose of this PR

<!-- Provide a clear and concise description of the changes. Explain the motivation behind these changes and link to relevant issues or discussions. -->
When dynamic allocation is enabled `Executor.Instances` is intentionally left as `nil` by the defaulting logic in 
`api/v1beta2/defaults.go`. However, `GetExecutorRequestResource` dereferences this field without a nil check, causing a panic when used with batch schedulers (like Volcano/kube-scheduler)

**Proposed changes:**
- Add nil check for `Executor.Instances` in `GetExecutorRequestResource` function
- Return empty `ResourceList{}` when Instances is `nil` 
- Add unit tests covering both nil and non-nil Instances scenarios


## Change Category

<!-- Indicate the type of change by marking the applicable boxes. -->

- [x] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)


## Checklist

- [x] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.


### Additional Notes
Verified in Kind cluster with Volcano scheduler - SparkApplication with `dynamicAllocation.enabled: true` and `batchScheduler: volcano` now processes successfully without panic

pullRequest.title: feat(webhook): Add webhook validation for SparkConnect CRD
pullRequest.body:
## Purpose of this PR

This PR introduces admission webhook validation for the SparkConnect CRD, aligning it with the existing validation patterns used for SparkApplication.

## Change Category

- [ ] Bugfix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [x] Feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that could affect existing functionality)
- [ ] Documentation update

## Rationale

The Spark Operator currently includes webhook validation for SparkApplication, but similar validation was not implemented for SparkConnect. This creates inconsistency and requires SDK-side validation that is better suited for controller-side enforcement.

This change:
- Aligns SparkConnect behavior with existing Spark CRDs
- Prevents invalid resource specifications at admission time
- Allows SDK-side validation logic to be simplified or removed

Validation rules implemented:
- Name must be DNS-1035 compliant (required for Service creation)
- SparkVersion is required
- Pod templates require Spark version >= 3.0.0
- DynamicAllocation: minExecutors <= maxExecutors
- DynamicAllocation: initialExecutors within min/max range
- Memory format validation (e.g., 1g, 512m, 1024k)

Helm chart has been updated and `make manifests` has been run to regenerate webhook configs.

## Checklist

- [x] I have conducted a self-review of my own code.
- [ ] I have updated documentation accordingly.
- [x] I have added tests that prove my changes are effective or that my feature works.
- [x] Existing unit tests pass locally with my changes.

### Additional Notes

Closes https://github.com/kubeflow/spark-operator/issues/2848.

pullRequest.title: feat(controller): add --kube-api-qps and --kube-api-burst flags
pullRequest.body:
# Purpose of this PR

Add `--kube-api-qps` and `--kube-api-burst` flags to the controller `start` command, allowing operators to configure the Kubernetes client rate limiter instead of relying on the hardcoded client-go defaults (QPS=5, burst=10).

In large-scale environments with hundreds of concurrent SparkApplications, the default rate limit causes `context canceled` errors on status updates after `spark-submit`. 

Fixes: #2864 

**Proposed changes:**

- `cmd/operator/controller/start.go`: declare `kubeAPIQPS`/`kubeAPIBurst` vars, register `--kube-api-qps` and `--kube-api-burst` flags, apply to `rest.Config` before building the manager
- `charts/spark-operator/templates/controller/deployment.yaml`: wire the two new flags from Helm values
- `charts/spark-operator/values.yaml`: add `controller.kubeAPIQPS` (default: `5`) and `controller.kubeAPIBurst` (default: `10`)

Default values are unchanged — existing deployments are unaffected.
